growth_records.cite: Sharma et al. Spatial segregation and cooperation in radially expanding microbial colonies under antibiotic stress. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-00982-2. [PMC8443724]
growth_records.snippet: the slope of the appearance time vs. log(density) plot provides an estimate of 1/k. For the conditions we measured, k ~ 0.02 min-1, which corresponds to an approximate doubling time of 35 min, only slightly slower than drug-free growth in l
growth_records.notes: Effective per-capita exponential growth rate k ~ 0.02 min-1 derived from slope of colony appearance-time vs log(inoculum density). growth_rate_per_h = 0.02 min-1 x 60 = 1.2 h-1. Doubling time = ln2/0.02 = 34.7 min = 0.578 h (paper states ~35 min). Measured on 1% BHI agar at 37 C, both with and without super-MIC ampicillin (expansion velocity approx invariant across drug conditions). No substrate uptake or product secretion measured. Colony/range-expansion growth on solid agar (nearest culture_mode = batch).

growth_records.medium: Human colonic organoid (colonoid) natively formed mucus, no exogenous nutrients (bacteria inoculated in a salt solution)

up.units: not quantified; bacteria grow on host-derived nutrients with no exogenous carbon added

growth_records.cite: Mikhaleva, Sofya et al. Adaptation of Enterococcus faecalis to intestinal mucus revealed by a human colonic organoid model. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01304-25. [PMC13098192]
growth_records.snippet: After 6 h, the number of viable Ef cells increased approximately 50-fold, indicating favorable growth conditions. This corresponds to a doubling time of ~1 h, which is slower than in the brain-heart infusion (BHI) medium, suggesting that Ef
growth_records.notes: Only quantitative growth parameter in the paper. ~50-fold increase in viable CFU over 6 h. Paper states doubling time ~1 h. Cross-check: mu = ln(50)/6 h = 3.912/6 = 0.652 /h; doubling time = ln2/0.652 = 1.06 h (~1 h, consistent with authors). growth_rate_per_h reported here as 0.65 (calculated from 50-fold/6 h); doubling_time 1.0 h as stated. No uptake or secretion rates measured (Tn-seq + live imaging study). culture_mode set to 'co-culture' as the strict list has no 'organoid/tissue-associated' option; it is a bacterium-on-host-tissue association. Medium is native colonoid mucus, composition not defined. BHI comparison is from a cited reference (#42), not measured here, so not recorded.

growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: Strains were grouped by growth rate (GR) into fast growing strains (GR > 1.5 h-1, E. faecalis KB1, B. animalis YL2, C. innocuum I46 and B. coccoides YL58) ... lactate production was confirmed for E. faecalis KB1 ... E. faecalis KB1 depleted
growth_records.notes: Growth rate stated only as a threshold bin (fast, GR>1.5 h-1); exact per-strain value is in Table S2 (supplement not available here), so 1.5 h-1 is a LOWER BOUND, not the measured value. Secretion/uptake are qualitative (produced/consumed/depleted) from untargeted MS of spent AF medium; no numeric mmol/gDW/h rates reported in main text. E. faecalis KB1 acidified medium (pH_SM<6.2). No Yxs/qS derivable (no biomass or substrate mass balance given).

growth_records.agit: 300 rpm (stir bar) for monoculture growth curves; Hungate tubes otherwise
growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Enterococcus faecalis KB1 (Firmicutes), Max OD 1.78, Mean μ ± SD 2.5 ± 0.01.
growth_records.notes: μ=2.5 h^-1 (±0.01) reported — fastest grower in the consortium. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/2.5 = 0.277 h. Max OD600 = 1.78. Arginine supplementation did not improve growth (text).

growth_records.cite: Hallinen, Kelsey M et al. Delayed antibiotic exposure induces population collapse in enterococcal communities with drug-resistant subpopulations. eLife (2020). DOI:10.7554/eLife.52813. [PMC7159880]
growth_records.method: calculated from reported doubling time (30-40 min)
growth_records.snippet: the maximum bacterial growth rate under these conditions, which corresponds to a doubling time of approximately 30-40 min... media was refreshed and waste removed at a rate of mu0 ~ 0.1 hr-1
growth_records.notes: Marginal record: this is an antibiotic population-dynamics study, not a GEM/MFA physiology paper. Drug-free maximum growth: doubling time ~30-40 min reported. mu = ln2/td: using td=0.583 h (35 min midpoint) -> mu = 0.693/0.583 = 1.19 h-1 (calculated; range mu=1.04 at 40 min to 1.39 at 30 min). Chemostat media-refresh rate mu0~0.1 h-1 is the imposed dilution/flow rate (an order of magnitude below drug-free growth rate), NOT a measured growth rate. No substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates reported (BHI complex medium). E. faecalis, 37 C, BHI. Included for completeness only.

growth_records.cite: Cermak, Nathan et al. High-throughput measurement of single-cell growth rates using serial microfluidic mass sensor arrays. Nature Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1038/nbt.3666. [PMC5064867]
growth_records.snippet: This strain behaved similarly to the gram-negative rod E. coli in the serial SMR array, growing at easily-detectable rates with a mean growth rate of 1.86 +/- 0.02/h.
growth_records.notes: Single-cell exponential growth rate measured directly by serial SMR array; mu = 1.86 +/- 0.02 /h reported (E. faecalis, Supplementary Fig. 12). Doubling time not reported; CALCULATED td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/1.86 = 0.3727 h = 22.4 min (growth_method reported for mu, doubling calculated). No uptake/secretion/MFA/yield data reported. E. faecalis grown overnight in Brain Heart Infusion (BHI, Difco) at 37C, diluted 100,000-fold in BHI + 0.2% Tween-80, incubated 3 h at 37C before loading. Base BHI composition not stated -> medium_composition_stated false. pH not stated.
